# KLiN Dual Wash Mitt

Price: £18.99

Color Options: Red, Blue
Variants: Red - £18.99, Blue - £18.99

## Description

The KL!N Dual Wash Mitt is a gentle and safe tool for washing painted surfaces. It works well with your preferred shampoo.

The mitt is 24x16cm (9.5"x6.5") in size and 3 inches thick. The super absorbent foam core holds a huge amount of wash solution, releasing it as you wash for swirl free washing.

The Dual Wash Mitt has a foldable cuff and a center opening. You can use it as a mitt or a pad, depending on your preference. This makes it the best of both worlds!

Uses:
- Washing Exterior Paint & Glass

Care:
Hang dry after use (Do not leave wet in the bucket).
We recommend using Carbon Collective Restore Microfibre Wash (https://klin.co.uk/products/carbon-collective-restore-microfibre-wash), Specifically approved for use by KLiN Korea.
- Machine wash separately with a dedicated detergent and warm water.
- Hang dry.
- Do not use Fabric Softener or Bleach.
- Always inspect any towel before it comes into contact with a surface.

## Quality First - KLIN KOREA

# KLIN Softy Series Wash Mitt | Ultra Soft Car Wash Mitt

Price: $20.99 USD

Color Options: Black, Red

Free Shipping (conditions apply)
Quality Controlled: Rigorously tested for quality
Free Returns: Within 30 days of purchase (conditions apply)

## Description

The Softy Series Wash Mitts are hands down the best wash media we have ever used. These products are made of premium ultra-soft and plush microfiber that effortlessly glides across paint, and releases dirt easily. They will help to prevent marring or scratching even the most delicate of surfaces during the hand washing process. Not to mention they hold up amazing over time!

Mitt Size: 8" X 6"

Washing:
We advise using Gyeon Quartz Q2M Towel Wash (https://parkscarcare.net/products/gyeon-towelwash-500ml) to maintain the longevity and performance of KLIN microfiber products.
Machine wash separately using neutral detergent with warm water and hang dry.
DO NOT use fabric softeners or bleach.
